genericTimeline.details: The President/Governor issues the notification calling upon the constituencies to elect members.
Candidates file their nomination papers before the Returning Officer.
The Returning Officer examines the validity of nomination papers filed by candidates.
Candidates are given a specific window to withdraw their nominations.
Official period where parties and candidates reach out to voters (Jan Sampark, Rallies).
Voters visit polling booths to cast their votes using EVM-VVPAT machines.
Votes are counted and results are declared for each constituency.

registrationSteps.title: Check Eligibility
registrationSteps.desc: Ensure you are an Indian citizen and 18+ years old on the qualifying date.

registrationSteps.title: Form 6 Submission
registrationSteps.desc: Fill Form 6 on the Voter Service Portal (voters.eci.gov.in) or Voter Helpline App.

registrationSteps.title: Upload Documents
registrationSteps.desc: Upload your photo, age proof, and address proof (Aadhaar, Passport, etc.).

registrationSteps.title: BLO Verification
registrationSteps.desc: A Booth Level Officer (BLO) will visit your address for physical verification.

registrationSteps.title: EPIC Generation
registrationSteps.desc: Once approved, your Elector Photo Identity Card (EPIC) will be generated and posted.

eciVideos.title: How to cast Vote using EVM - VVPAT
eciVideos.desc: Official ECI step-by-step guide on voting with Electronic Voting Machines.

eciVideos.title: Understanding the EVM Security
eciVideos.desc: How Electronic Voting Machines are kept completely secure and tamper-proof.

eciVideos.title: Know Your Candidate (KYC App)
eciVideos.desc: Check candidate backgrounds and criminal records via official ECI apps.

items.name: Aadhaar Card
items.purpose: Universal proof of ID and address.

items.name: Passport
items.purpose: Official international ID and address proof.

flashcards.title: Largest Democracy
flashcards.fact: India has over 968 million registered voters.

flashcards.title: EVM Transparency
flashcards.fact: VVPAT machines allow voters to verify their vote.

flashcards.title: First Election
flashcards.fact: The first general elections were held in 1951-52.

English.welcome: Namaste! I am your Chunaav Assistant. How can I help you today?
English.fallback: I'm sorry, I can only help with specific topics like Voter ID, EVM, NOTA, and Registration.
